topic.name: Military Aircraft Development
topic.description: The documents discuss the design, development, and operational history of various military aircraft from different eras, focusing on their technological advancements and roles in warfare.

Document 1: Lockheed Have Blue was the code name for Lockheed's demonstrator that preceded the F-117 Nighthawk production stealth aircraft.

Document 2: The Petlyakov Pe-3 was the long-range, night fighter version of the successful Petlyakov Pe-2 high-speed bomber used by the Soviet Union during World War II.

Document 3: The Platt-LePage XR-1 was an early American twin-rotor helicopter, built by the Platt-LePage Aircraft Company.

Document 4: The Bell Boeing V-22 Osprey is an American multi-mission, tiltrotor military aircraft with both vertical takeoff and landing capabilities.

Document 5: The Sukhoi Su-9 was an early jet fighter built in the Soviet Union shortly after World War II.
The design was heavily influenced by captured German jet fighters and it was subsequently redesigned to use a Soviet copy of a German turbojet.
Document 6: The Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-3 was a Soviet interceptor and fighter aircraft used during World War II.
It was built in large numbers during 1941 before Factory No. 1 was converted to build the Ilyushin Il-2.
Document 7: The Petlyakov Pe-8 was a Soviet heavy bomber designed before World War II, and the only four-engine bomber the USSR built during the war.
Its primary mission was to attack German airfields, rail yards and other rear-area facilities at night.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the annual rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.

Oxford won by half a length in a time of 22 minutes and 39 seconds.
Document 4: The 71st Boat Race took place on 28 March 1914.
Cambridge won by four-and-a-half lengths in a time of 20 minutes 23 seconds.
Document 5: The 123rd Boat Race took place on 19 March 1977.
Oxford won by seven lengths, their biggest margin of victory for more than 90 years.
Document 6: The 47th Boat Race took place in 1890.

Document 7: The 121st Boat Race took place on 29 March 1975.
Cambridge won their seventh race in eight years by three-and-three-quarter lengths.
